final_embedding_text:
Context: This document is a MarketBeat report by Cushman & Wakefield, focusing on the industrial and warehousing real estate market in Bengaluru for the second half of 2024. It provides key market fundamentals, leasing volumes, supply trends, and rental growth across various submarkets for both industrial and warehousing segments.

Chunk Content: # MARKETBEAT BENGALURU INDUSTRIAL H2 2024

### MARKET FUNDAMENTALS

| Fundamental | H1 2024 | H2 2024 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Inventory (in MSF) | 43 | 45 |
| Vacancy (%) | 7% | 20% |
| Upcoming Supply in next 12 months (MSF) | 2.5 | 5.5 |
| L&I Leasing (in MSF) | 3.7 | 2.0 |
| Average Rentals Range (INR PSFPM) | 22 | 22.5 |
| Land Transacted (in acres) | 145 | 355 |
| Land Values Range (INR MN per acre) | 25-35 | 25-35 |

---

### WAREHOUSE LEASE VOLUMES AT A RECORD HIGH IN 2024
Bengaluru recorded 1.8 msf of warehousing lease volumes in H2 2024 with the full year ending at nearly 5 msf, a record high and around 17% higher than the previous year. The eastern corridor continued to dominate warehouse leasing activity, accounting for 39% of lease volumes in H2 with locations such as Hoskote, Malur and Soukya Road the major contributors. The western corridor followed in the second spot with a 35% share with Nelamangala the primary warehousing destination. South bengaluru (Hosur) accounted for 26% of lease volumes. 3PL operators contributed over 50% of warehouse lease volumes in h2 followed by retail (25%) and manufacturing (18%). For the full year, eastern corridor contributed 41% of annual warehouse lease volumes followed by west (38%) and south (20%). 3PL operators accounted for 41% of annual lease volumes followed by manufacturing (24%) and retail (18%).

### HEALTHY SUPPLY PIPELINE; RENTALS RECORD MARGINAL GROWTH
Bengaluru recorded warehouse supply addition of around 2 msf in H2 with the city’s total inventory (A+B) at 45 msf by the end of the year. Supply pipeline remains strong with around leading developers and warehouse operators expediting greenfield projects to cater to the robust demand for premium warehouse space. Land parcels have also been transacted by leading players across the western (Dabaspet, Nelamangala) and eastern (Hoskote, Narsapura) corridors for planned developments. Headline warehouse rentals posted a growth of 4-5% on an annual basis in H2 while land prices rose by 9-10% yoy.

### SLOWER INDUSTRIAL LEASING IN H2
Bengaluru recorded industrial leasing of just over 0.2 msf in H2, with the full year figure at around 0.8 msf, an 18% drop from the previous year. Industrial leasing was recorded at Doddaballapur (north) in H2 and for the full year, north Bengaluru contributed 41% of annual leasing volumes followed by west (34%). Headline industrial rentals increased by 3-4% on an annual basis in H2.

---

| Submarket | Industrial Rent (INR/SF/MO) | Industrial Rent Y-O-Y % Growth |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Bommasandra | 30-33 | 3-4% |
| Peenya & Yeshwanthpur | 42-45 | 3-4% |
| Kumbalgodu | 23-25 | 2-3% |
| Narsapura | 25-28 | 3-4% |
| Doddaballapur | 21-23 | 3-4% |

**Caption:** INDUSTRIAL RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH. Units: Industrial Rent in INR PSFPM (bars), Growth in % (diamonds).

---

| Submarket | Warehousing Rent (INR/SF/MO) | Warehousing Rent Y-O-Y % Growth |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Hosur Road (Jigani, Attibele) | 24-27 | 3-4% |
| Nelamangala | 22-25 | 4-5% |
| Bidadi | 22-25 | 3-4% |
| Hoskote | 23-26 | 4-5% |
| Devanahalli/KIADB... | 30-32 | 4-5% |

**Caption:** WAREHOUSING RENT / Y-O-Y GROWTH. Units: Warehousing Rent in INR PSFPM (bars), Growth in % (diamonds).

It includes data on rents, land rates, and significant transactions for the second half of 2024.

Chunk Content: # BENGALURU INDUSTRIAL H2 2024

### KEY WAREHOUSING SUBMARKETS – RENTALS AND LAND RATES – DECEMBER 2024

| SUBMARKET | WAREHOUSE RENT (INR/SF/MONTH) | WAREHOUSE RENT Y-O-Y CHANGE | LAND RATES (INR MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (US$ MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES (EURO MN/ACRE) | LAND RATES Y-O-Y CHANGE |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Hosur Road (Jigani, Attibele) | 24-27 | 3-4% | 70-120 | 0.83-1.42 | 0.78-1.33 | 9-10% |
| Nelamangala | 22-25 | 4-5% | 35-50 | 0.41-0.59 | 0.39-0.56 | 9-10% |
| Bidadii | 22-25 | 3-4% | 55-65 | 0.65-0.77 | 0.61-0.73 | 7-8% |
| Hoskote, Malur | 23-26 | 4-5% | 35-40 | 0.41-0.47 | 0.39-0.45 | 9-10% |
| Devanahalli/KIADB Hitech Zone | 30-32 | 4-5% | 60-100 | 0.71-1.18 | 0.67-1.12 | 8-9% |

| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Bommasandra | 30-33 | 3-4% | 175-195 | 2.07-2.31 | 1.95-2.18 | 8-10% |

| Kumbalgodu | 23-25 | 2-3% | 110-130 | 1.30-1.54 | 1.23-1.45 | 6-8% |
| Narsapura | 25-28 | 3-4% | 25-30 | 0.30-0.36 | 0.28-0.33 | 8-10% |
| Doddaballapur | 21-23 | 3-4% | 25-35 | 0.30-0.41 | 0.28-0.39 | 8-10% |

Quoted land rates for serviced industrial land parcels are mentioned
# Represents government owned serviced plots in industrial parks
Percentage growth are in local currency; Y-O-Y – Year on Year
Conversion Rate: US$1= 84.4 INR and Euro 1 = INR 90.1
